forecasts.reasoning: The recall involves a very small amount of highly perishable product (303 pounds of chicken caesar wraps) that had "Best By" dates (Feb 21-25) which already expired around the time the recall was issued (Feb 24). This means the physical product recovery and disposal process will conclude almost immediately. However, the exact resolution criteria depend on the USDA FSIS officially changing the recall status from "Active" to "Closed" on their website. The administrative process for the recalling firm to submit final documentation and for FSIS to review, conduct effectiveness checks, and formally close the recall typically takes several months. While the very small scope and short shelf life might expedite things, 97 days (late February to June 1) is a relatively short timeframe for resolving government bureaucratic processes. I estimate a 38% probability that it closes before the June 1, 2025 deadline.

The background reveals that World Atlas has already updated the article title for the Gulf of Mexico to include "(Gulf of America)". This shows intent and awareness from the website's maintainers to reflect the January 2025 executive order renaming the body of water. However, the specified URL for the North America page (a legacy .htm structure) likely uses a static image map. While they have updated the text elsewhere, updating static map images across the site can be a slow, manual process, and there is no guarantee they will prioritize updating this specific map before the July 1, 2025 deadline. Because Google Maps and other third-party API map providers have already made the change, if the page embeds a dynamic map, it might automatically update; however, the prompt notes that at the time of the question, the map still displayed "Gulf of Mexico," indicating it either relies on a static image or a non-Google map service that hasn't updated for this site. Given the short timeframe (under two months from the knowledge cutoff) and the effort required to update legacy image maps, the probability is moderate but less than 50%.

Beyond Meat's primary financial burden is $1.15 billion in 0% convertible notes, which do not mature until March 2027. Because these notes have no regular coupon payment, Beyond Meat is not at risk of an imminent interest payment default. The primary risk of bankruptcy in the short term was running out of operational cash to pay vendors and ongoing expenses.

However, immediately prior to the knowledge cutoff (on May 7, 2025), Beyond Meat announced it had successfully closed on a financing facility providing up to $100 million in new senior secured debt from an affiliate of the Ahimsa Foundation. This deal also included warrants for the lender to purchase up to 12.5% of outstanding shares, strongly indicating an out-of-court rescue meant to preserve the company's equity and avoid Chapter 11. 

With the forecast cutoff being May 8, 2025, and the resolution date being July 1, 2025, there are less than 8 weeks remaining in the time horizon. It is exceptionally rare for a company to file for bankruptcy within mere weeks of securing a $100 million liquidity lifeline explicitly intended for general corporate purposes and growth initiatives. Given the recent cash infusion and the lack of debt maturities before 2027, the likelihood of a bankruptcy petition being filed before July 1, 2025, is exceedingly low.

The question asks if the International Traditional Karate Federation (ITKF) will announce that Egypt has been chosen to host the 2026 Traditional Karate World Championship before July 1, 2025.
In late April 2025 (specifically April 29), the Egyptian Minister of Youth and Sports, Ashraf Sobhi, met with the President of the ITKF, Gilberto Gaertner, to discuss Egypt submitting a formal bid to host the 2026 World Championship. The fact that the ITKF President traveled to Egypt for these discussions barely two months before the resolution date strongly indicates that Egypt is in the late stages of securing the hosting rights. 
Furthermore, the text from the ITKF website homepage (cached around late April/early May 2025) explicitly states, "As host nation and reigning world champion, Egypt outlines the concept, technical responsibility, and institutional ambition behind the organization of...". It appears Egypt is either already considered the host nation for this upcoming event or an announcement is imminent. 
Because the meeting just occurred and the ITKF usually finalizes hosting decisions shortly after such high-level state visits and negotiations, it is highly probable that the official announcement will be formalized and published before July 1, 2025. Given the explicit quote from the ITKF site designating Egypt as "host nation", the likelihood is very high. I estimate an 87% probability.

Sheikh Hasina fled to India in August 2024 after being ousted, and faces severe legal charges and an extradition request (filed December 2024) from the interim Yunus government. Extraditing a former head of state is a lengthy and politically fraught process, and India has shown no willingness to send her back to face trial; they are much more likely to continue harboring her or facilitate her move to a third country. While Hasina issued rhetorical statements in April 2025 vowing to return, there is no practical or political mechanism for her to do so safely without a massive, sudden counter-coup, which currently shows no signs of materializing. Given that her return would almost certainly result in immediate arrest, and considering the very narrow 50-day window (May 12 to July 1, 2025), the probability of her entering Bangladeshi territory during this specific period is exceptionally low. I am forecasting the minimum allowed probability of 5%.
